prompt: Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print, Hokusai style, 1830s. A dramatic mountain landscape with multiple winding footpaths descending from jagged peaks toward a single low valley floor. A tiny lone figure on each path follows the steepest downward slope, all paths converging toward the deepest lowest point in the valley. Flat unmodulated areas of indigo blue, pale cream, burnt ochre. Bold black outlines. Visible woodgrain texture throughout. Asymmetric composition. No gradients, no photographic shading, no Western perspective. Stark figure-ground contrast. Traditional cartouche border. No text, no labels
tradition_approach: Flat unmodulated color blocks in indigo, ochre and cream. Bold black outlines. Visible woodgrain texture. Asymmetric mountainscape composition with converging downward paths mapping the loss landscape traversal.
